channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Natural disasters and political instability hampered U.S. museum attendance in 2025
item.description: The Art Newspaper's latest annual study of "the world's 100 most visited art museums" also reveals signs of modest growth.
item.content:encoded:
The Art Newspaper's latest annual study of "the world's 100 most visited art museums" also reveals signs of modest growth.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for April 4, 2026: With Not My Job guest Olivia Munn
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Negin Farsad, special guest Olivia Munn and panelists Rachel Coster, Peter Grosz, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Negin Farsad, special guest Olivia Munn and panelists Rachel Coster, Peter Grosz, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson

(Image credit: Rodin Eckenroth)

item.title: 'London Falling': A teenage imposter, an aging gangster and a body in the Thames
item.description: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.content:encoded: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: The busiest place you've never seen
item.description: Photographer Julia Gunther and writer-filmmaker Nick Schönfeld chronicle the rhythms of daily life on Tristan da Cunha, the world's most remote inhabited island.
item.content:encoded:
Photographer Julia Gunther and writer-filmmaker Nick Schönfeld chronicle the rhythms of daily life on Tristan da Cunha, the world's most remote inhabited island.

(Image credit: Nick Schönfeld for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Julia Gunther

item.title: Buttercream wool and jelly bean eyes: The art of the Easter lamb cake
item.description: The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.
item.content:encoded:
The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.

(Image credit: Charra Jarosz)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Dr. Sanjay Gupta explains what we do — and still don't — know about pain
item.description: "Pain is a mysterious thing," says neurosurgeon Dr. Sanjay Gupta. But understanding how it works in the body and different kinds of treatment can help you find the right pain relief when you need it.
item.content:encoded: "Pain is a mysterious thing," says neurosurgeon Dr. Sanjay Gupta. But understanding how it works in the body and different kinds of treatment can help you find the right pain relief when you need it.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Author George Saunders shares the experience he wishes he could give to every person
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: 'The Drama' is a half-baked comedy about wedding stress and cancel culture
item.description: Robert Pattinson and Zendaya play an engaged couple whose happiness is derailed when a boozy game of "What's the Worst Thing You've Ever Done?" uncovers a dark secret from the past.
item.content:encoded: Robert Pattinson and Zendaya play an engaged couple whose happiness is derailed when a boozy game of "What's the Worst Thing You've Ever Done?" uncovers a dark secret from the past.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Comic/filmmaker and star Julio Torres spins immigration stress into satire
item.description: Torres talks about the obstacles he faced in the immigration system after he came to the U.S. from El Salvador in his 20s. His new HBO Max show is Color Theories. Originally broadcast March 11, 2024.
item.content:encoded: Torres talks about the obstacles he faced in the immigration system after he came to the U.S. from El Salvador in his 20s. His new HBO Max show is Color Theories. Originally broadcast March 11, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Dark wedding comedy 'The Drama' has a provocative premise — but it never commits
item.description: Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.

(Image credit: A24)
item.dc:creator: Aisha Harris
